business.headline: From April to March: Here are all the key FY27 tax deadlines you must trackThe tax calendar may seem long, but staying aware of these dates can make things much simpler. Instead of rushing at the last minute, spreading out your compliance and investments across the year can help you avoid stress and penalties.

Gold down over Rs 1,200, silver slips Rs 6,000: Is this a dip worth buying?Precious metals have slipped noticeably, with gold and silver both under pressure. For investors, this raises a familiar dilemma, i.e., is this the right moment to step in or stay on the sidelines?

Motherson clarifies on Noida workers' protest, says driven by misinformationIn a filing to stock exchanges, the company said recent developments reported in the media relate to labour protests in Noida and some other cities, and are not specific to any one company.

Retail inflation rises to 3.40% in March from 3.21% in FebruaryThe year-on-year inflation rate, based on All India Consumer Price Index (CPI), stood at 3.40% (Provisional) in March 2026. This is slightly higher than February's 3.21%, but still below the 4% mark that is often seen as manageable.

8th Pay Commission meetings: Should govt employees expect a pay revision soon?After months of speculation, the 8th Pay Commission process has picked up pace. With meetings now scheduled, the big question remains, i.e., should government employees expect a pay revision anytime soon?

Sensex ends 700 points lower, Nifty below 23,900; auto stocks hit hardMarkets may have steadied after early losses, but rising oil prices and geopolitical risks continue to cloud sentiment, keeping investors cautious and leaving the near-term outlook uncertain and volatile.

Maruti, Eicher Motors, other auto stocks tumble: What's behind the fall?Maruti Suzuki fell over 4%, while Eicher Motors dropped nearly 5%, making them among the top losers on the Nifty 50. Other auto names also saw weakness, even as the broader market pared some of its early losses.

Ambanis lead Asia's 10 richest families in 2026: Check who else is on the listAt number one is the Ambani family, with a net worth of about $89.7 billion. Their flagship company, Reliance Industries, continues to expand across energy, telecom, retail and financial services.

When employers evade, enforcement looks awaySystemic gaps in enforcement, coupled with deliberate non-compliance, leave workers outside legal protection, raising a larger question: whether labour laws exist merely on paper or are meant to be enforced.

New labour codes explained: How your salary, PF, and gratuity will changeEmployees should ask four key questions: What's my actual wage base? Is PF on capped or full wages? Is this just a salary split change? And am I gaining long-term benefits?

Sensex pares losses by more than 800 points, Nifty recovers from early crashStock market crash: The recovery follows a steep fall at the open, when the Sensex had plunged over 1,600 points and the Nifty slipped below key levels in early trade, reflecting a weak start to the week.

DA hike coming: How much will your monthly pay rise-Rs 360, Rs 540 or more?As the DA hike nears, expectations are building. Even a modest increase can add a steady boost to your salary, but how much can you really expect this time? Let's have a look.

Jyoti CNC shares tumble up to 16%: What's behind the sharp fall?The sharp fall follows reports that French authorities have initiated a judicial probe into the company's subsidiary, Huron Graffenstaden SAS, over alleged violations linked to export control norms.

Stagflation fears are back: How should investors position their money in 2026?During stagflation, money gets re-allocated in the market hence few of the asset classes which usually do not come across as attractive options may become the flavour of the season.

US plans Hormuz blockade: Will India's crude oil and LPG supply be hit?India is already facing pressure on fuel and LPG supplies amid the West Asia conflict. With the US now planning a Hormuz blockade, could the situation worsen?

Sensex tumbles 1,600 points: What you should know about today's market crashMarkets are reacting to oil above $100 and rising Hormuz tensions. If the US blockade begins and shipping risks escalate, banks, the rupee and foreign flows could face sustained pressure.

Sensex tumbles 1,600 points as US Hormuz blockade threat pushes crude above $100Stock market crash: The main indices on Dalal Street opened sharply lower as the Sensex and Nifty crashed ahead of a US blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, with crude oil already surging above $100 per barrel.

Forgotten fortune: Why assets worth Rs 2 lakh crore remain unclaimed in IndiaUnclaimed assets across bank deposits, shares, insurance policies and retirement funds have crossed Rs 2 lakh crore. This article explains why this money remains out of reach and what makes it so hard to recover.

Buying a car or bike? The hidden truth behind insurance sold at dealershipsWhen you buy a car or bike, the final bill usually comes as an 'on-road price' that includes insurance. For most buyers, this is accepted without much thought, as it appears to be a standard part of the purchase. But what often goes unnoticed is that this insurance is not mandatory to buy from the dealer.

Middle East war to cut global growth, spike inflation despite truce: World BankThe World Bank's baseline estimate now projects growth in emerging markets and developing economies of 3.65 per cent in 2026, compared to 4 per cent in October, dropping as low as 2.6 per cent in an adverse scenario with a longer-lasting war.

entertainment.headline: Ranveer Singh comforts Asha Bhosle's grieving granddaughter Zanai at last rites An emotional scene at Asha Bhosle's funeral in Mumbai saw actor Ranveer Singh consoling her granddaughter Zanai. The legendary singer was honoured with state rites on Monday.

Big Mistakes review: Dan Levy's family comedy is a messy mix that mostly worksRelease Date:Thu Apr 09, 2026 Big Mistakes brings Dan Levy back in full chaotic form. This time, he plays a small-town pastor juggling secrets, a wildly dysfunctional family, and a crime mess that spirals fast. With spot-on performances, and biting humour, it's a messy, twisted, and very bingeable ride.

When will you live: Shocked Sadhguru questions Alia Bhatt's 9-hour sleep habit Actor Alia Bhatt's candid chat with Sadhguru turned into an unexpected debate on sleep and living fully. The spiritual leader also urged her to take cues from her daughter's joyful approach to life.

Priya Sachdev marks 1st anniversary after Sunjay Kapur's death: Our story isn't over Priya Sachdev Kapur marked her ninth wedding anniversary with late husband Sunjay Kapur by sharing emotional memories on Instagram. She posted wedding photos and a handwritten note, expressing that their bond continues beyond his death.

Sitarist Rishabh Rikhiram Sharma's Abhi Na Jao tribute to Asha Bhosle in Kolkata Rishab Rikhiram Sharma delivered an emotional tribute to Asha Bhosle during a live concert in Kolkata, leaving audiences visibly moved.

How Asha Bhosle helped Padmini Kolhapure land her first film Padmini Kolhapure fondly recalled how the late singer Asha Bhosle, a family member, helped her land her first film role. Asha Bhosle, honoured with state awards, died at 92, on April 12.

Rest in peace, Asha tai: Iconic singer cremated with full state honours in Mumbai Thousands gathered on the streets of Mumbai to bid a final farewell to Asha Bhosle. The legendary singer was cremated at the Shivaji Park Crematorium.

Vaazha 2 enters top 6 Malayalam grossers with over Rs 150-crore haul The recent Malayalam sensation Vaazha II continues its steady run at the global box office. It has now entered the top 6 Malayalam grossers with over Rs 150 crore.

Asha Bhosle once revealed her last wish, and it sums up her 83 years in music Asha Bhosle once shared a deeply personal wish about how she wanted to say goodbye, a sentiment that now feels especially poignant. As the legendary voice falls silent at 92, her words offer a moving glimpse into a life devoted entirely to music.

Left us an entire encyclopedia of eternal music: Big B's tribute to Asha Bhosle Amitabh Bachchan led tributes to Asha Bhosle, calling her death the end of an era. The legendary singer died at the age of 92.

Saaz, the film that triggered talk around Asha Bhosle's bond with Lata didi Sai Paranjpye's 1998 film, Saaz, traced the relationship between two sisters who become successful singers. Were Aruna Irani and Shabana Azmi's roles inspired by Lata Mangeshkar and Asha Bhosle in any way?

Ilaiyaraaja calls Asha Bhosle sister in video tribute: Great talent, unique voice Veteran music composer Ilaiyaraaja expressed deep grief over the loss of iconic singer Asha Bhosle. He fondly remembered their collaborations and prayed for her lasting legacy.

Dhurandhar's Aditya Uppal recalls watching Sanjay Dutt on VCR, then acting with him Actor Aditya Uppal recalled watching Sanjay Dutt on VCR and shared his experience of acting with him in Dhurandhar: The Revenge. He also named Farhan Akhtar as his dream collaborator.

Jr NTR flexes his muscles, stuns fans with new pic ahead of NTRNeel shoot Jr NTR's toned physique for his upcoming film, tentatively titled Dragon, has sparked widespread excitement among fans and the film fraternity. The makers have clarified there are no delays in shooting, with the project progressing as planned.

Actor Kasthuri admits watching leaked Jana Nayagan clips: 3-hour propaganda for TVK Actor Kasthuri Shankar admitted to viewing leaked clips of Jana Nayagan, labelling it a political propaganda film supporting Vijay's party, TVK. The leak had sparked controversy just before the Tamil Nadu assembly elections.

Ramayana heads to CinemaCon 2026: Yash to attend, posters stir buzz online The upcoming film Ramayana is gearing up for a major showcase at CinemaCon 2026 in Las Vegas, with Yash expected to be in attendance. Several photos of the film's posters displayed at the venue have surfaced online.

Dhurandhar 2's 25-day run: Biggest Indian grosser excluding China, Gulf box office Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge is rewriting box office rules, emerging as the top Indian grosser when China and Gulf earnings are taken out of the equation. As the Ranveer Singh-starrer storms past Rs 1,700 crore worldwide, its unprecedented run signals a major shift in how global success is defined for Indian cinema.

Perfect Crown first impressions: All glitter, little gold Perfect Crown, starring IU and Byeon Woo-seok, blends royal intrigue with rom-com elements but needs stronger writing to engage fully, says our review.

Euphoria 3 puts OnlyFans debate in focus: Cast members say 'not for us to judge' Euphoria returns with its third season, delving into the complexities of digital-age sex work and OnlyFans culture. At a global roundtable, actors Martha Kelly and Chloe Cherry talked about OnlyFans, whether it is empowering or exploiting women.

general news.headline: US begins blockade of all Iranian ports in Strait of Hormuz, risking wider war Live Updates The blockade may put pressure on Iran, but it also risks unsettling global markets and pulling the US deeper into a conflict that could drag on, given that Iran has shown no willingness to concede on nuclear enrichment and control of Hormuz.

Any Iranian ship that comes close to US blockade will be eliminated: TrumpA fresh warning from Trump escalates tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, as a US naval blockade disrupts shipping, sends oil prices soaring, and raises fears of a supply shock not seen in decades.

Did team leads run religious conversion racket, prey on women at TCS Nashik?Allegations of sexual exploitation and forced religious conversion have surfaced from IT major TCS's Nashik facility. Multiple FIRs have been lodged, and several people, including team leads and engineers, have been arrested. The alleged harassment is said to have continued for two to three years.

Three scenarios as a planned US Hormuz blockade risks huge global falloutThe US's move to control the Strait of Hormuz risks bringing in major powers and disrupting the fragile balance of energy flows.

Can US last 3 months, asks Iran, says has oil for sale in tankers for 90 daysIran mocked the US, asking whether Washington and its allies could "endure three months" of blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. The Iranian Embassy in Bulgaria in a post on X said Tehran had oil in tankers on high seas that was enough to sell for 90 days.

Live: Noida firm says workers' protest driven by misinformation about wagesNoida Labour Protest Live Updates: A wage protest by labour unions turned violent in Noida on Monday morning, with workers torching vehicles, clashing with police, blocking roads and vandalising factories, triggering massive traffic jams across Noida and parts of Delhi, leaving commuters stranded during peak office hours. Authorities deployed additional forces to contain the unrest as workers from multiple industrial units pressed for long-pending salary hikes. Commuters faced delays reaching schools and offices, with autos and cabs scarce or charging nearly triple fares. Similar protests were seen in Faridabad, involving Motherson Group staffers, and spread to parts of Rajasthan, including Bhiwadi, where factory workers demanded higher wages. Follow indiatoday.in for live updates.

Iran envoy says Indian ships didn't have to pay Hormuz toll: Ask your governmentIran's ambassador to India, Mohammad Fathali, said Indian ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz have not paid any tolls, highlighting strong ties between New Delhi and Tehran amid escalating regional tensions.

You attended RSS-linked events 4 times: Arvind Kejriwal to judge in courtFormer Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal alleged that Delhi High Court judge Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma had attended events organised by the Adhivakta Parishad, a lawyers' body affiliated with the RSS, on four occasions. He further said he strongly opposed the BJP-RSS ideology.

Trump posts AI image of himself as Jesus Christ, internet spots EpsteinUS President Donald Trump shared a Christ-like image of himself after launching a sharp attack on Pope Leo XIV, calling him "weak" and "very liberal" over his stance on global conflicts.

SRH vs RR Live: Ishan Kishan misses out on hundred, departs for 91Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) vs Rajasthan Royals (RR) Live Score, IPL 2026: SRH (145/3 in 14 ovs). Ishan Kishan missed out on a well-deserved hundred as Sandeep Sharma struck to remove the SRH stand-in captain. Earlier, Kishan's blazing fifty and an 88-run stand with Heinrich Klaasen for the third wicket had pulled Sunrisers Hyderabad back into the contest after the early loss of Travis Head and Abhishek Sharma against Rajasthan Royals at Uppal.

Supreme Court denies interim voting rights to lakhs deleted from Bengal rollsWith over 34 lakh appeals pending and polling days away, the Supreme Court faces a high-stakes dilemma, can voter rights be protected without overwhelming the system or risking errors in the electoral process?

Rs 90 lakh or no wedding night, UP bride says; family tries to burn groom aliveThe demand left the groom's family stunned. When the money was not given, the bride allegedly took her jewellery and returned to her parents' home.

When will you live: Shocked Sadhguru questions Alia Bhatt's 9-hour sleep habitActor Alia Bhatt's candid chat with Sadhguru turned into an unexpected debate on sleep and living fully. The spiritual leader also urged her to take cues from her daughter's joyful approach to life.

Live: 1 injured in Israel after Hezbollah launches fresh strikesUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Talks between Iran and the US faltered on Sunday after US Vice President JD Vance said that Tehran did not accept the US's terms to end the war. Vance left for the US after the talks hit a roadblock. Iran, on the other hand, dubbed the talks a failure and alleged that the US tried to demand excessively. The first phase of US-Iran talks in Islamabad concluded with both sides exchanging written texts to confirm agreements, even as earlier face-to-face meetings marked the start of direct negotiations. Limited progress was reported on key issues, including possible restrictions on strikes in southern Lebanon and movement on unfreezing Iranian assets. Iran has maintained hard red lines, insisting on a Lebanon ceasefire and broader conditions, while tensions remain high with US mine-clearing operations underway in the Strait of Hormuz and warnings issued to American vessels.

Indian workers wearing cameras to train AI? Viral clips spark factory fearsViral videos of Indian factory workers wearing head-mounted cameras have sparked fears that companies may be using real human labour to train AI systems. The clips have triggered a wider debate around job security, ethics, and the future of automation.

No Gulf port will be safe: Iran's big warning hours before US blockade beginsIran warned that no port in the Persian Gulf or Sea of Oman would remain safe if its own ports are targeted, as the US prepares to enforce a blockade on Iranian maritime activity. The move follows failed talks, with tensions escalating, shipping disrupted in the Strait of Hormuz, and oil prices surging amid fears of wider regional fallout.
Sangam, Spectrum, Scandals and The Poes SurgeThe DMK had not yet fallen. But it had begun to look like a banyan tree whose roots were now tripping its own devotees.

What Tamil Nadu candidates list tells us: Clasp of caste is permanentTamil Nadu's 2026 election candidate lists reveal how deeply caste-based calculations dominate politics, overriding constitutional ideals and merit in candidate selection.

f(f(O))>f(O): What does Iran's mathematical warning to US over Hormuz mean?In a post on X, Iran's Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf mocked the US's warning of a naval blockade on ships linked to Iranian ports, suggesting the move could ultimately rebound on American consumers by driving up fuel costs.

Rest in peace, Asha tai: Iconic singer cremated with full state honours in MumbaiThousands gathered on the streets of Mumbai to bid a final farewell to Asha Bhosle. The legendary singer was cremated at the Shivaji Park Crematorium.

After anchovies and prawn curry, a royal menu with korma, puran poli goes viralFirst, an old royal menu went viral and sparked a debate. Now, another menu from the same era is doing the rounds on the internet, serving up a different side of the story.

Door not closed yet: Mediators hopeful of US-Iran talks before truce endsIran-US talks: Turkish and Egyptian foreign ministers held separate calls with their Pakistani counterpart on Sunday. Both then spoke with White House envoy Steve Witkoff and Araghchi, sources told Axios.

Netanyahu's call to Vance derailed US-Iran talks? Araghchi reveals inside detailsIran-US talks in Pakistan ended without agreement after 21 hours, with Tehran alleging that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's phone call to JD Vance in Islamabad disrupted negotiations.

India likely to see below-average monsoon this year as El Nino returnsWhile most parts of India will experience below-normal rainfall, some areas in Northeast, Northwest and South Peninsular India, are likely to face normal to above-normal monsoon.

sports.headline: Replace him with Sooryavanshi for India: Fans slam Abhishek after duck vs RR Fans called for Abhishek Sharma to be axed from the Indian T20I setup and replace him with Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in the side. Abhishek was dismissed for a first-ball duck during the match at Uppal.

SRH vs RR Playing XIs: Pretorious, Hinge headline shake-up for Uppal showdown SRH vs RR: Both teams ring in major changes with three debutants set to feature, including Pretorious, Hinge and Sakib, as Rajasthan opt to bowl first in their IPL clash in Hyderabad.

Will Varun be ready for CSK clash? KKR provides update ahead of Chepauk tie Kolkata Knight Riders have provided an update on the fitness of Varun Chakravarthy ahead of their clash against the Chennai Super Kings on Tuesday, April 14. Varun has been out of action for the last two games for KKR.

IPL 2026: GT vs CSK fixtures swapped due to Ahmedabad civic polls The Board of Control for Cricket in India has tweaked the Indian Premier League schedule, swapping fixtures between Gujarat Titans and Chennai Super Kings due to civic polls in Gujarat.

Ghanaian footballer killed in team bus after armed robbery attack A Ghanian footballer, Dominic Frimpong was shot dead after armed robbers attacked the team bus in Ghana. The incident has raised fresh concerns over player safety during domestic travel.

After reclaiming No.1 spot, Jannik Sinner sets sight on French Open glory Jannik Sinner has set his sights on winning the French Open title after going past Carlos Alcaraz to become the new World No.1 in tennis. Sinner defeated Alcaraz at the Monte Carlo Masters on Sunday to climb to the top spot.

Two Indian sports, cricket, and hockey, will be at 2030 CWG: Sports Minister Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya on Monday said the 2030 Commonwealth Games in Ahmedabad are likely to feature two indigenous Indian sports, with cricket and hockey confirmed as part of the programme.

Tim David punished for disobeying umpires' instruction; Hardik Pandya reprimanded Tim David was fined 25 per cent of his match fees for ignoring the umpire's instructions in the first innings of the MI vs RCB clash. Meanwhile, Hardik Pandya has also been fined for slow over-rate.

SRH missing Cummins captaincy: Zaheer Khan slams interim skipper Ishan Kishan Zaheer Khan slammed Ishan Kishan's stint as the interim captain of SRH, claiming that the team is missing the presence of Pat Cummins in the side. Kishan has taken over as the interim captain till Cummins returns.

Hockey: Sports Ministry, Fulton differ on India's plans for Asiad, World Cup Sports Ministry pushes for separate squads for Asian Games and World Cup to avoid burnout, but coaches prefer sending full-strength teams to both events.

SRH vs RR Live: Ishan Kishan misses out on hundred, departs for 91 Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) vs Rajasthan Royals (RR) Live Score, IPL 2026: SRH (145/3 in 14 ovs). Ishan Kishan missed out on a well-deserved hundred as Sandeep Sharma struck to remove the SRH stand-in captain. Earlier, Kishan's blazing fifty and an 88-run stand with Heinrich Klaasen for the third wicket had pulled Sunrisers Hyderabad back into the contest after the early loss of Travis Head and Abhishek Sharma against Rajasthan Royals at Uppal.

Wahab Riaz pushes for women's cricket league in Pakistan on PSL modelWahab Riaz, mentor of Pakistan women's cricket team, has urged the Pakistan Cricket Board to launch an international T20 women's league in the country, modelled on the Pakistan Super League.

MI sweating over Rohit fitness, Mumbai star set for scans on hamstring issueRohit Sharma is set to undergo scans to determine the extent of the hamstring injury he suffered during MI's loss to RCB at the Wankhede stadium on Sunday, April 12. Rohit suffered the issue during the sixth over of MI's chase.

Women's T20 World Cup: ICC announces record prize pool, winners to get USD 2.34 mnWith just 60 days remaining until the start of the 2026 ICC Women's T20 World Cup, the International Cricket Council has announced a record prize money pool for the tournament, which will be held in England and Wales.

Guwahati groundstaff rewarded Rs 25 lakh for incredible job in rain-hit gamesThe Guwahati leg of the IPL was completed despite persistent rain threats, thanks to the efforts of the groundstaff. The Assam Cricket Association rewarded the curators and staff with Rs 25 lakh for ensuring matches went ahead smoothly.

Formula 1 will return to India in 2027: Sports Minister Mansukh MandaviyaIndia is pushing hard for Formula 1's return, but the road back remains complicated. While Mansukh Mandaviya is confident of 2027, the final call still rests with Formula One.

Neymar gets World Cup boost as Carlo Ancelotti keeps Brazil door openNeymar received a timely boost in his bid to make Brazil's FIFA World Cup squad, as head coach Carlo Ancelotti insisted that the door remains open for the veteran forward.

Sachin Tendulkar breaks down in tears during Asha Bhosle's last rites in MumbaiSachin Tendulkar was seen in tears during Asha Bhosle's last rites in Mumbai, as a viral clip captured his emotional moment with her family. The visuals reflected their close, long-standing bond beyond public life and shared admiration.

48 years later…Sawan Barwal outruns the legend of Shivnath SinghSawan Barwal's dramatic finish at the Rotterdam Marathon did more than break a 48-year-old national record. It lifted a psychological weight that had long hung over Indian distance running. In surpassing Shivnath Singh's mark, Barwal has opened the door for a new generation to push the limits further.

Her legacy will live on for generations: Brett Lee's tribute to Asha BhosleBrett Lee paid a heartfelt tribute to Asha Bhosle after her passing, remembering her warmth beyond her legendary voice. Reflecting on their collaboration, he said her legacy will live on for generations and continue to inspire countless artists worldwide.

Candidates: Vaishali's slip-up throws title race open, will she hold her nerve?Candidates 2026: Vaishali Rameshbabu had a golden chance to cement her lead and head into the final rest day of the Candidates 2026 tournament. But her 12th-round loss has ensured that there is everything to play for in the last two rounds, and her chances of featuring in the World Chess Championship have taken a bit of a jolt.

IPL a movie? PSL batter's remark leaves Jacob Oram in splits at presserJacob Oram was left in splits during a Pakistan Super League press conference after being asked about Rilee Rossouw's recent remark that the IPL is "more of a movie than actual cricket," struggling to hold back his laughter at the comparison.

Why is Jasprit Bumrah not taking wickets, and should Mumbai Indians be worried?Jasprit Bumrah's 122-ball wicketless stretch in the Indian Premier League has caused concern among the Mumbai Indians fans. What's causing it? We at India Today, took a deepdive.

Can Italy still qualify for World Cup? Iran-US feud sparks hope for Gli AzzuriItaly's World Cup hopes may be hanging by a thread, but an unexpected global twist has kept the door slightly open. With uncertainty around Iran, the Azzurri are suddenly back in the conversation.

Struggling Sunrisers brace for Sooryavanshi challenge against in-form RoyalsIPL 2026, SRH vs RR: Rajasthan Royals have won all their matches thus far and will be looking to carry their winning run when they face an indifferent-looking Sunrisers Hyderabad on Monday.

Should MI play Rutherford ahead of out-of-form Tilak? RCB loss raises questionsIPL 2026: Mumbai Indians' team balance is under the spotlight after another setback, with Tilak Varma's form raising questions. Even as calls grow for change, Sherfane Rutherford has backed the top order to deliver.

Tall boy, tall hopes: Ayush Shetty's run in China shows he is here to stayBadminton Asia Championships 2026: At 6ft 4in, Ayush Shetty is no longer just a prospect. His sensational run in China proved he belongs at the top, signalling his arrival as a serious contender.

Phil Salt not overthinking IPL 2026 form: Focused on impact over milestonesIPL 2026: Phil Salt's return to form was not about numbers, but about impact when it mattered most. After a slow start, his Wankhede blitz showed exactly why RCB back him at the top.

One Pandya had to win: Krunal relishing RCB's win over MI on Wankhede homecomingIPL 2026: Krunal Pandya's Wankhede homecoming turned into a statement night as he got the better of brother Hardik. In a high-scoring clash, his control with the ball made the difference for RCB.

IPL Play of the Day: Lessons learnt as RCB's Suyash Sharma fuels Wankhede conquestIPL 2026: RCB's smarter use of Impact Subs keeps their bowling intact as Suyash Sharma's attacking spell breaks MI's chase and powers a statement win at Wankhede

Virat Kohli takes an autorickshaw on a Mumbai highway meant for FerrarisIPL 2026, MI vs RCB: Virat Kohli found himself out of sync during a run-fest in Mumbai on Sunday. While his teammates struck at 200-plus, the King's 38-ball fifty left him frustrated.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
